ontology.foodex2_description: Wheat grass juice — plant-derived beverage from Triticum aestivum young shoots, blended with water and herbal infusions
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps precisely to herbal wheatgrass juice as a food item; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

allergens: gluten (potential — wheatgrass from Triticum aestivum; cross-contamination risk)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard glass of herbal wheatgrass juice is approximately 300 g (300 ml), as commonly served at Singaporean health-beverage stalls and juice bars.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Wheat grass juice, raw (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for wheatgrass juice used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values for energy (20 kcal/100 g), protein (0.83 g), fat (0.17 g), carbohydrate (4.0 g), sugar (2.0 g), dietary fibre (1.33 g), sodium (5 mg), calcium (10 mg), iron (0.5 mg), cholesterol (0 mg), saturated fat (0.03 g), trans fat (0 g), and vitamin C (5 mg) are consistent with published wheatgrass juice composition data. Herbal infusion component is assumed minor and nutritionally negligible at typical dilution. Singapore HPB Foodo database consulted but no exact wheatgrass juice entry found; USDA SR Legacy used as closest approved-source equivalent.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Vegetable and Grass Juices — analogous estimate based on low-carbohydrate green vegetable juice data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for herbal wheatgrass juice in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at approximately 15 (low) by analogy with other low-carbohydrate green vegetable juices (e.g., spinach juice, celery juice) documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. Carbohydrate content is 4.0 g/100 g; at a 300 g serving, available carbohydrate is approximately 12 g. Glycemic load per serving = (15 × 12) / 100 = 1.8 (very low). This estimate is conservative and defensible given the low sugar and high chlorophyll content of wheatgrass.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (5 mg/100 g) and potassium ~120 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Herbal wheatgrass juice is suitable for individuals with hypertension when consumed unsweetened; its very low sodium content (5 mg/100 g) and moderate potassium (~120 mg/100 g) support a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio that aids blood pressure management — request no added sugar or honey to avoid elevating caloric and sugar load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (4 g/100 g), low sugar (2 g/100 g), estimated GI ~15, glycemic load per 300 g serving ~1.8
type2_diabetes.guidance: Herbal wheatgrass juice presents a very low glycemic load (~1.8 per 300 g serving) and is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sweeteners; preliminary evidence suggests wheatgrass chlorophyll and enzymes may support insulin sensitivity, but this beverage should complement — not replace — a structured dietary management plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.03 g/100 g), zero trans fat, zero cholesterol, and antioxidant-rich wheatgrass
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Herbal wheatgrass juice is cardioprotective in profile — negligible saturated fat (0.03 g/100 g), no trans fat, no cholesterol, and vitamin C (5 mg/100 g) with chlorophyll-derived antioxidants support endothelial health; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume it unsweetened to avoid added-sugar contributions to triglyceride load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Herbal Wheatgrass Juice is a minimally processed green beverage made by blending or cold-pressing fresh young wheatgrass (Triticum aestivum) shoots with water and, in some preparations, additional herbal infusions such as pandan, moringa, or other botanicals. It is commonly sold at Singaporean health-food stalls, juice bars, and wellness cafes, typically served chilled in a 300 ml glass. The flavour is distinctly grassy, mildly bitter, and earthy, with a thin, watery texture and vivid green colour from chlorophyll. Nutritionally, it is a low-calorie beverage (approximately 20 kcal per 100 g) with modest protein (0.83 g), very low fat (0.17 g), low carbohydrate (4 g), and dietary fibre (1.33 g). Sodium is very low at 5 mg/100 g, making it suitable for sodium-restricted diets. Vitamin C (5 mg/100 g), iron (0.5 mg/100 g), and calcium (10 mg/100 g) are present in modest amounts. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 15 (low), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 1.8 per standard 300 g serving — making it appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. Wheatgrass is rich in chlorophyll, flavonoids, and antioxidant enzymes that have been associated with anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective effects in preliminary research. It is vegan, vegetarian, and dairy-free, though individuals with wheat or gluten sensitivity should exercise caution due to potential cross-contamination during harvesting. This beverage is widely consumed as a morning health tonic or post-exercise recovery drink in Singapore and across Southeast Asia.

confidence_reason: No exact wheatgrass juice entry was found in the Singapore HPB Foodo database. Nutrient values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for wheatgrass juice and are plausible for a diluted blended preparation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using comparable low-carbohydrate green vegetable juices. Confidence is medium rather than high because the herbal infusion component is unspecified and the exact preparation ratio (wheatgrass to water) is not confirmed from an approved source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for wheatgrass juice is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific herbal wheatgrass juice preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-derived beverage with no industrial fat processing). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-based beverage contains no cholesterol). Input cultural tags (Filipino, Noodle, Coffee, Cake) were disregarded as clearly erroneous for a wheatgrass juice product; cuisine reclassified as Singaporean Health Beverage with country_of_origin Singapore. Gluten-free dietary flag set to false despite input claim: wheatgrass is derived from Triticum aestivum and carries cross-contamination risk; individuals with coeliac disease should verify with the vendor. Potassium value of 120 mg/100 g is an estimate consistent with published wheatgrass juice composition data from USDA SR Legacy and is documented in nutrient_source notes.
